title: cordyline rumphii
intro: Cordyline rumphii, also known as Cheeseman's or Red Palm Lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cordyline rumphii are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and provide antioxidant effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves and rhizomes of the plant are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or capsules for use in traditional medicine.
